notes: Won't even start launcher
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Proton 3.7.7 beta: by installing dotnet40 & corefonts with an older version of wine the launcher is now accessible but only the combat demo works. Main game fails to connect with a "CRC" error.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Game is now working on wine staging https://youtu.be/JcDY4WFENug and CRC fail can be worked around with a simple registry fix. Game still freezes in steam proton hence borked, here's instructions on how to get the launcher and game to start: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150#issuecomment-431715919
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Got it working in Steam Proton... BUT it'll crash when more than one CPU core is set to the process. Steps to get it to run (in the reproduction section of my report) plus using "taskset -c 0 %command%" in steams launch options you can get into the game but it'll be very laggy with one core: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150#issuecomment-431715919
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Followed the details of this open issue (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150). It's working, but having to use taskset -c 0 %command% limits the game to a single core and creates a big bottleneck that makes it difficult to play. At 1080p Ultra settings I was getting 20-25 fps.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Won't launch whatsoever.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes:
Requires a custom Proton patched by RedMcG listed here as well as a dotnet40 install through winetricks: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150#issuecomment-436502763
These patches may end up being pulled into Proton main branch soon, there are some isolated issues with some system configurations.. AMD specifically will have issues with horizons unless they spoof as Nvidia with a DXVK config (which is already be fixed in the next DXVK release).
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: I try all tweaks from github. Launcher doesn't start.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Manual install of Dotnet40 and used Proton 3.16-4 Beta - for Elite Dangerous from https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Had to download the patched proton at https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta, but had to extract it to ~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d/ instead of the directory suggested on GitHub. Then installed dotnet40 into the WINEPREFIX at <steamlibrary>/steamapps/compatdata/359320/pfx/ and was good to go. Everything works nicely, HOTAS was detected and I get stable 60 fps at 4k.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Used Proton Patched by RedMcg (https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta), and installed dotnet40 using Protontricks from https://github.com/Sirmentio/protontricks.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Used RedMcG's patched proton from https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta and protontricks from https://github.com/Sirmentio/protontricks to install dotnet40 on the WINEPREFIX. On my Radeon 290X, it can run at 1080p maximum. 1440p and 4k makes the framerate drop to 2-5 FPS, so silver for the moment.
title: Elite Dangerous

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon R9 390
notes: I used patched proton 3.16-4 by redmcg(https://github.com/redmcg/wine). And also installed dotnet40 using protontricks(https://github.com/Sirmentio/protontricks). Got 40-70fps maxed out 4k(I disabled AA cause at 4k it's really not needed.)
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Proton 3.16-4 Beta - for Elite Dangerous from https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta.  Installed dotnet452 with winetricks.  Needed dxvk >= 0.92 to work around AMD shader problems for Horizons.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: As Mentioned in other reports i had to use the custom ED build for Proton. I also needed to put it in ~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d/ instead of a subdirectory in ~/.steam/ after that i had to increase my file descriptor limit which is outlined on the github page.
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: All working perfectly after using the patched 3.16-4ED proton, also managed to get Saitek X56 HOTAS fully functional (I needed to recalibrate the XY axis first in the official joystick software, and used Qemu + Win8 VM to do so.)
title: Elite Dangerous

notes:
- GloriousEggroll's patched Proton:  https://drive.google.com/open?id=1fuHiinA6UFnhbpIUDXawMN96Dz69mBNh

- winetricks = vcrun2017 (everything else needed is already patched on that proton)

- Workaround for endless planet generation on AMD GPUs:  https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/issues/36#issuecomment-435696138

- Workaround for CRC Failed error: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150#issuecomment-433647095

notes:
It does not work with any proton-version shipped with steam.
There is a custom proton-version with an installation guide at https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-5_Beta
Using that proton build and following the instructions, it works perfectly.

notes:

"https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-4_Beta"

title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Does not even start to launcher without excruciating workaround (see other comments).
title: Elite Dangerous

notes: Running perfectly fine after switching to forked Proton build "3.16-5 Beta ED": https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/tag/ED_Proton_3.16-5_Beta - check install section. I had to do the following: #1. Open Terminal  #2. mkdir -p ~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d && cd ~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d && wget https://github.com/redmcg/wine/releases/download/ED_Proton_3.16-5_Beta/Proton_3.16-5_Beta_ED.tar.gz && tar xfv Proton_3.16-5_Beta_ED.tar.gz && cd Proton\ 3.16-5\ Beta\ ED/ && ./fixED.sh #3. In Steam check "Settings" -> "Steam Play" and in menu "Compatibility tool" select "Proton 3.16-5 Beta ED" #4. Restart Steam #5. Play "Elite Dangerous" (this took pretty long even on a beefier system) #6. Login to your account and start the game as usual. ... see you in game, commanders o7
title: Elite Dangerous

notes:
The game seems to run fine with the Beta-version for ED.
Same FPS as under WIndows with identical Graphic-Settings.
However, It doesn't feel completely as smooth as under windows, when looking around or moving.
But its generaly good playable.
Good work so far. Thx.
title: Elite Dangerous
